revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{統合文字|祇}} {{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 |画像= |所在地=南丹市園部町大河内 |位置= |祭神=[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] 、...」
revision.text:
{{統合文字|祇}} {{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 |画像= |所在地=南丹市園部町大河内 |位置= |祭神=[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] 、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[天暦]]３年（[[949年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。
==祭神== *[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] （すさのおのみこと） *[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]] （いざなぎのみこと） *[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] （いざなみのみこと）
==歴史== 社伝によれば949年（[[天暦]]３年）、 [[藤原純友]]の弟である純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。また、1374年（[[文中]]３年）には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]が現在の地に遷座したものと伝わっている。
== 境内 == 社殿のうち'''本殿'''は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営であり、 [[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士、下村義視と田井義高が再建したものである。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、本殿は [[重要文化財]]に指定されている。
== 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]]園部町大河内溝ノ上5
'''交通アクセス''' * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]] からバスで25分（京阪京都交通バス八田線） *ぐるりんバス「るり渓橋」バス停下車 徒歩で10分
== 参考文献 == *『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社 - 京都府観光連盟] *[https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]]

{{参照方法|date=2017年9月13日 (水) 09:08 (UTC))}} {{統合文字|祇}} {{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 |画像= |所在地=南丹市園部町大河内 |位置= |祭神=[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] 、[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]、[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[天暦]]３年（[[949年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。
==祭神== *[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] （すさのおのみこと） *[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]] （いざなぎのみこと） *[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] （いざなみのみこと）
==歴史== 社伝によれば949年（[[天暦]]３年）、 [[藤原純友]]の弟である純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。また、1374年（[[文中]]３年）には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]が現在の地に遷座したものと伝わっている。
== 境内 == 社殿のうち'''本殿'''は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営であり、 [[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士、下村義視と田井義高が再建したものである。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、本殿は [[重要文化財]]に指定されている。
== 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]]園部町大河内溝ノ上5
'''交通アクセス''' * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]] からバスで25分（京阪京都交通バス八田線） *ぐるりんバス「るり渓橋」バス停下車 徒歩で10分
== 参考文献 == *『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社 - 京都府観光連盟] *[https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]]

== 境内 == 社殿のうち'''本殿'''は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営であり、 [[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士、下村義視と田井義高が再建したものである。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、本殿は [[重要文化財]]に指定されている。

'''交通アクセス''' * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]] からバスで25分（京阪京都交通バス八田線） *ぐるりんバス「るり渓橋」バス停下車 徒歩で10分

{{参照方法|date=2017年9月13日 (水) 09:08 (UTC))}} {{統合文字|祇}} {{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 |画像= |所在地=[[京都府]][[南丹市]]園部町大河内 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大山祇神社}} |祭神=[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]
[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]
[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[天暦]]3年（[[949年]]） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[天暦]]3年（949年）、[[藤原純友]]の弟の純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。また、[[文中]]3年（1374年）には楠木正成の弟の[[楠木正季|正季]]が現在の地に遷座したとも伝わる。 == 境内 == 社殿のうち本殿は[[応永]]26年（1419年）の造営で、[[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士の下村義視と田井義高の再建とされる。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（附 形板12枚）（建造物） - 昭和33年（1958年）5月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]]園部町大河内溝ノ上5 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]]から、路線バス（京阪京都交通バス八田線）で約25分 * ぐるりんバス「るり渓橋」バス停下車 （徒歩約10分） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社] - 京都府観光連盟 * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]]

revision.comment: 「おおやま”ず”みじんじゃ」が正しい記述。歴史の加筆修正
revision.text:
{{統合文字|祇}} {{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 |画像= |所在地=[[京都府]][[南丹市]]園部町大河内 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 3 | 緯度秒 = 16.71 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 22.95 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto |祭神=[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]
[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]
[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=949年（[[天暦]]3年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） ==歴史== 社伝によれば、949年（[[天暦]]3年）、[[藤原純友]]の弟の純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。その後、1181年（[[養和]]元年）11月に源頼一{{refnest|group=注釈|[[角川日本地名大辞典]]には、源'''領一'''と記述されている。}}が祇園の牛頭天王を合祀し、1374年（[[文中]]3年）[[9月15日]]には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]によって、現在の地に遷座したと伝わる『角川日本地名大辞典 27 大阪府』角川書店、1982年。。 1740年（[[元文]]5年）に[[園部藩]]が行った調査報告書「寺社類集」によれば、環状年号は未考であり、祭神は[[祇園牛頭天王]]、[[熊野三所権現]]、[[蔵王権現|金峰蔵王権現]]とされている。また、社蔵記録からこの頃は開明大山大社と呼称されていたことが判るが、1870年（[[明治]]3年）に京都府庁が実施した調査にて、社名を大山祇神社と回答したことから現在の呼称が定着したものと推測される。 == 境内 == 社殿のうち本殿は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営で、[[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士の下村義視と田井義高の再建とされる。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、[[重要文化財]]に指定されている『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（附 形板12枚）（建造物） - 1958年（昭和33年）5月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内溝ノ上5 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]]から、[[京阪京都交通]]バス八田線に乗車しバス停「八田」下車後、ぐるりんバスに乗り換える。バス停「るり渓橋」下車後、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社] - 京都府観光連盟 * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

{{統合文字|祇}} {{神社| |名称 = 大山祇神社| |画像 =| |所在地 = [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]][[園部町大河内|大河内]]溝ノ上| |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 3|緯度秒 = 16.71|N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N| |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 24|経度秒 = 22.95|E(東経)及びW(西経) = E| |地図国コード = JP| |地図 = Japan Kyoto| |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]
[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]
[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]| |社格 = 旧[[村社]]| |創建 = 949年（[[天暦]]3年）| |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]| |別名 = 開明山田大社（旧称）| |札所等 =| |例祭 =| |神事 =| }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]][[園部町大河内|大河内]][[大河内溝ノ上|溝ノ上]][[船井郡]][[大河内村 (京都府船井郡)|]]{{small|大字}}溝ノ上、[[西本梅村]]{{small|大字}}大河内、船井郡園部町{{small|大字}}大河内にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、949年（[[天暦]]3年）、[[藤原純友]]の弟の純索が紀州[[熊野権現]]を祀ったのが始まりとされる。その後、1181年（[[養和]]元年）11月に[[源頼一]]{{refnest|group=注釈|[[角川日本地名大辞典]]には、源'''領一'''と記述されている。}}が祇園の牛頭天王を合祀し、1374年（[[文中]]3年）[[9月15日]]には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]によって、現在の地に遷座したと伝わる『角川日本地名大辞典 27 大阪府』角川書店、1982年。。 1740年（[[元文]]5年）に[[園部藩]]が行った調査報告書「寺社類集」によれば、環状年号は未考であり、祭神は[[祇園牛頭天王]]、[[熊野三所権現]]、[[蔵王権現|金峰蔵王権現]]とされている。また、社蔵記録からこの頃は開明大山大社と呼称されていたことが判るが、1870年（[[明治]]3年）に京都府庁が実施した調査にて、社名を大山祇神社と回答したことから現在の呼称が定着したものと推測される。 == 境内 == 社殿のうち本殿は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営で、[[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士の下村義視と田井義高の再建とされる。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、[[重要文化財]]に指定されている『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（附 形板12枚）（建造物） - 1958年（昭和33年）5月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]][[園部町大河内|大河内]]溝ノ上5 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]]から、[[京阪京都交通]]バス八田線に乗車しバス停「八田」下車後、ぐるりんバスに乗り換える。バス停「るり渓橋」下車後、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社] - 京都府観光連盟 * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:熊野信仰]] [[Category:熊野神社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:長良流]] [[Category:源氏]] [[Category:楠木氏]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=949年（[[天暦]]3年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） ==歴史== 社伝によれば、949年（[[天暦]]3年）、[[藤原純友]]の弟の純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。その後、1181年（[[養和]]元年）11月に源頼一{{refnest|group=注釈|[[角川日本地名大辞典]]には、源'''領一'''と記述されている。}}が祇園の牛頭天王を合祀し、1374年（[[文中]]3年）[[9月15日]]には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]によって、現在の地に遷座したと伝わる『角川日本地名大辞典 27 大阪府』角川書店、1982年。。 1740年（[[元文]]5年）に[[園部藩]]が行った調査報告書「寺社類集」によれば、勧請年号は未考であり、祭神は[[祇園牛頭天王]]、[[熊野三所権現]]、[[蔵王権現|金峰蔵王権現]]とされている。また、社蔵記録からこの頃は開明大山大社と呼称されていたことが判るが、1870年（[[明治]]3年）に京都府庁が実施した調査にて、社名を大山祇神社と回答したことから現在の呼称が定着したものと推測される。 == 境内 == 社殿のうち本殿は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営で、[[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士の下村義視と田井義高の再建とされる。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、[[重要文化財]]に指定されている『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社。 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（附 形板12枚）（建造物） - 1958年（昭和33年）5月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内溝ノ上5 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]]から、[[京阪京都交通]]バス八田線に乗車しバス停「八田」下車後、ぐるりんバスに乗り換える。バス停「るり渓橋」下車後、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社] - 京都府観光連盟 * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]
[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=949年（[[天暦]]3年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） ==歴史== 社伝によれば、949年（[[天暦]]3年）、[[藤原純友]]の弟の純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。その後、1181年（[[養和]]元年）11月に源頼一{{refnest|group=注釈|[[角川日本地名大辞典]]には、源'''領一'''と記述されている。}}が祇園の牛頭天王を合祀し、1374年（[[文中]]3年）[[9月15日]]には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]によって、現在の地に遷座したと伝わる『角川日本地名大辞典 27 大阪府』角川書店、1982年。。 1740年（[[元文]]5年）に[[園部藩]]が行った調査報告書「寺社類集」によれば、勧請年号は未考であり、祭神は[[祇園牛頭天王]]、[[熊野三所権現]]、[[蔵王権現|金峰蔵王権現]]とされている。また、社蔵記録からこの頃は開明大山大社と呼称されていたことが判るが、1870年（[[明治]]3年）に京都府庁が実施した調査にて、社名を大山祇神社と回答したことから現在の呼称が定着したものと推測される。 == 境内 == 社殿のうち本殿は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営で、[[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士の下村義視と田井義高の再建とされる。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、[[重要文化財]]に指定されている『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社。 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（附 形板12枚）（建造物） - 1958年（昭和33年）5月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町]]大河内溝ノ上5 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]]から、[[京阪京都交通]]バス八田線に乗車しバス停「八田」下車後、ぐるりんバスに乗り換える。バス停「るり渓橋」下車後、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社] - [[京都府観光連盟]] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Created page with "== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the Sado Gold Mi..."
revision.text: == Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. [[category:new pages]][[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]]

* shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. [[category:new pages]][[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == 祭神 == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **Location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == 祭神 == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **Location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]]

Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]]

Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
