Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deities= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == 境内 == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 境内" to "== Shrine facilities"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= **Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri** (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly **Kaimyō Yamada Taisha** (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in **Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture**. Under the **former shrine ranking system**, it was classified as a **village shrine** (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, **Fujihara no Sumimasa** (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the **Kumano Gongen** from **Kii Province** in **949 CE (Tenryaku 3)**, marking the shrine's foundation. In **1181 CE (Yōwa 1)**, **Minamoto no Yorikazu**{{refnest|group=Note|The **Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary** records this name as **Minamoto no Ryōichi**.}} enshrined **Gozu Tennō** from **Gion Shrine**, merging it with the shrine. Later, in **1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)**, **Kusunoki Masatsuna**, the younger brother of **Kusunoki Masashige**, relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In **1740 CE (Genbun 5)**, an **investigation report by the Sonobe Domain** titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as **Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha**. However, in **1870 CE (Meiji 3)**, a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as **Ōyamazumi Shrine**, which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current **Main Hall (Honden)** was rebuilt in **1419 CE (Ōei 26)** by **Shimomura Yoshimi** and **Tai Yoshitaka**, who were said to be descendants of the **Kusunoki clan**. The structure preserves characteristics of **medieval shrine architecture** and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a **National Important Cultural Property**."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * **Main Hall (Honden)** (附 形板12枚) – **Designated on May 14, 1958** (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == **location:** * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 **Access:** * **Train & Bus:** Take the **JR West** {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to **Sonobe Station**, then transfer to the **Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)**. Get off at **Hatta** and switch to the **Gururin Bus**. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. Get off at **Rurikei Bridge** and walk **10 minutes**. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Get off at '''Rurikei Bridge''' and walk '''10 minutes'''. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}}[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en}}[[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en|12=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] }} [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] [[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)}}

{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内|12=draft}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦|12=draft}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友|12=draft}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|12=draft}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=draft}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|12=draft}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線|12=draft}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Get off at '''Rurikei Bridge''' and walk '''10 minutes'''. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|12=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en|12=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] }} [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] [[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内|12=draft}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} |shrine_rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦|12=draft}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友|12=draft}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|12=draft}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=draft}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|12=draft}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線|12=draft}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Get off at '''Rurikei Bridge''' and walk '''10 minutes'''. == References == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|12=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en|12=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] [[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)}}

{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft|12=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine Ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=draft|12=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友|12=draft|12=draft}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|12=draft|12=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|12=draft|12=draft}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=draft|12=draft}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線|12=draft|12=draft}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Get off at '''Rurikei Bridge''' and walk '''10 minutes'''. == References == === Note === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - revision.comment: 18 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市) up to rev 99835022
revision.text:
{{統合文字|祇}} {{神社 |名称=大山祇神社 |画像=[[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
本殿（国の[[重要文化財]]、中央の朱塗りの建物） |所在地=[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]] | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 3 | 緯度秒 = 16.71 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 22.95 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto |祭神=[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]
[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]
[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=949年（[[天暦]]3年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） ==歴史== 社伝によれば、949年（[[天暦]]3年）、[[藤原純友]]の弟の純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。その後、1181年（[[養和]]元年）11月に源頼一{{refnest|group=注釈|[[角川日本地名大辞典]]には、源'''領一'''と記述されている。}}が祇園の牛頭天王を合祀し、1374年（[[文中]]3年）[[9月15日]]には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]によって、現在の地に遷座したと伝わる『角川日本地名大辞典 27 大阪府』角川書店、1982年。。 1740年（[[元文]]5年）に[[園部藩]]が行った調査報告書「寺社類集」によれば、勧請年号は未考であり、祭神は[[祇園牛頭天王]]、[[熊野三所権現]]、[[蔵王権現|金峰蔵王権現]]とされている。また、社蔵記録からこの頃は開明大山大社と呼称されていたことが判るが、1870年（[[明治]]3年）に京都府庁が実施した調査にて、社名を大山祇神社と回答したことから現在の呼称が定着したものと推測される。 == 境内 == 社殿のうち本殿は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営で、[[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士の下村義視と田井義高の再建とされる。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、[[重要文化財]]に指定されている『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社。 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（附 形板12枚）（建造物） - 1958年（昭和33年）5月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]]溝ノ上5 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]]から、[[京阪京都交通]]バス八田線に乗車しバス停「八田」下車後、ぐるりんバスに乗り換える。バス停「るり渓橋」下車後、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社] - [[京都府観光連盟]] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

本殿（国の[[重要文化財]]、中央の朱塗りの建物） |所在地=[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]] | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 3 | 緯度秒 = 16.71 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 22.95 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kyoto |祭神=[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]
[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]
[[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]] |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=949年（[[天暦]]3年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |別名= 開明山田大社（旧称） |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]（すさのおのみこと） * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） ==歴史== 社伝によれば、949年（[[天暦]]3年）、[[藤原純友]]の弟の純索が紀州熊野権現を祀ったのが始まりとされる。その後、1181年（[[養和]]元年）11月に源頼一{{refnest|group=注釈|[[角川日本地名大辞典]]には、源'''領一'''と記述されている。}}が祇園の牛頭天王を合祀し、1374年（[[文中]]3年）[[9月15日]]には楠木正成の弟である[[楠木正季|正季]]によって、現在の地に遷座したと伝わる『角川日本地名大辞典 27 大阪府』角川書店、1982年。。 1740年（[[元文]]5年）に[[園部藩]]が行った調査報告書「寺社類集」によれば、勧請年号は未考であり、祭神は[[祇園牛頭天王]]、[[熊野三所権現]]、[[蔵王権現|金峰蔵王権現]]とされている。また、社蔵記録からこの頃は開明大山大社と呼称されていたことが判るが、1870年（[[明治]]3年）に京都府庁が実施した調査にて、社名を大山祇神社と回答したことから現在の呼称が定着したものと推測される。 == 境内 == 社殿のうち本殿は1419年（[[応永]]26年）の造営で、[[楠木氏]]の子孫とされる郷士の下村義視と田井義高の再建とされる。中世の神社建築の特徴を残して良く保存されており、[[重要文化財]]に指定されている『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』、平凡社。 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|拝殿 File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|鳥居 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿（附 形板12枚）（建造物） - 1958年（昭和33年）5月14日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]]溝ノ上5 '''交通アクセス''' * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[園部駅]]から、[[京阪京都交通]]バス八田線に乗車しバス停「八田」下車後、ぐるりんバスに乗り換える。バス停「るり渓橋」下車後、徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 大山祇神社] - [[京都府観光連盟]] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html 南丹市園部の文化財] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:南丹市の建築物]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine Ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Get off at '''Rurikei Bridge''' and walk '''10 minutes'''. == References == === Note === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|おおやまつみじんじゃ}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] [[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)}} {{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|version=99835022|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)}} {{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|version=99835022|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] [[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine Ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku era|ja|天暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine Ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|ja|藤原純友|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|ja|南丹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|ja|嵯峨野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Get off at '''Rurikei Bridge''' and walk '''10 minutes'''. == References == === Note === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) -
