Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Gozu Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570247}} - {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} - {{ill|Zaō Gongen|lt=Zaō Gongen|lt=Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen|qq=}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. Main Hall (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}, the central vermilion building) |location= {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|WD=Q170421|lt=Nantan, Kyoto|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Sonobe, Nantan|ja|園部町大河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonobe, Nantan|1=Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q128021473}} |coordinates= {{coord|35|3|16.71|N|135|24|22.95|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} |shrine_Rank= Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= 949 CE ({{ill|Tenryaku|en|Tenryaku|WD=Q1346281|lt=Tenryaku era|1=Tenryaku}} 3) |main_hall= '''Ikken-sha Nagare-zukuri''' (一間社流造) |alternate_names= Formerly '''Kaimyō Yamada Taisha''' (開明山田大社) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Ōkawachi, Sonobe-chō, Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture'''. Under the '''former shrine Ranking system''', it was classified as a '''village shrine''' (村社). == deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} (Izanagi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} (Izanami-no-Mikoto) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Fujihara no Sumimasa''' (the brother of {{ill|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|en|Fujiwara no Sumitomo|WD=Q1187731|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo|1=Fujiwara no Sumitomo}}) enshrined the '''Kumano Gongen''' from '''Kii Province''' in '''949 CE (Tenryaku 3)''', marking the shrine's foundation. In '''1181 CE (Yōwa 1)''', '''Minamoto no Yorikazu'''{{refnest|group=Note|The '''Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary''' records this name as '''Minamoto no Ryōichi'''.}} enshrined '''Gozu Tennō''' from '''Gion Shrine''', merging it with the shrine. Later, in '''1374 CE (Bunchū 3, September 15)''', '''Kusunoki Masatsuna''', the younger brother of '''Kusunoki Masashige''', relocated the shrine to its present site."Kadokawa Japan Place Name Dictionary: Osaka Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1982. In '''1740 CE (Genbun 5)''', an '''investigation report by the Sonobe Domain''' titled *"Shaji Ruishū"* (寺社類集) recorded that the shrine’s founding date was uncertain. At the time, the deities enshrined were: - {{ill|Gion Gozu Tennō|ja|祇園牛頭天王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Gozu Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570247}} - {{ill|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sanzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} - {{ill|Zaō Gongen|lt=Zaō Gongen|lt=Zao Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zao Gongen|qq=}} Additionally, records from this period refer to the shrine as '''Kaimyō Ōyama Taisha'''. However, in '''1870 CE (Meiji 3)''', a survey conducted by the Kyoto Prefectural Office recorded the shrine’s name as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', which led to the modern name becoming standardized. == Shrine facilities == The current '''Main Hall (Honden)''' was rebuilt in '''1419 CE (Ōei 26)''' by '''Shimomura Yoshimi''' and '''Tai Yoshitaka''', who were said to be descendants of the '''Kusunoki clan'''. The structure preserves characteristics of '''medieval shrine architecture''' and remains in excellent condition. Due to its historical and architectural significance, it has been designated a '''National Important Cultural Property'''."Japan Historical Place Name Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture," Heibonsha. File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan), torii.jpg|Torii Gate == Cultural Properties == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * '''Main Hall (Honden)''' (附 形板12枚) – '''Designated on May 14, 1958''' (Shōwa 33){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2056|大山祗神社本殿}}. == 現地情報 == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|WD=Q170421|lt=Nantan, Kyoto|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, Sonobe-chō Ōkawachi Mizunoue 5 '''Access:''' * '''Train & Bus:''' Take the '''JR West''' {{ill|Sagano Line|en|Sagano Line|WD=Q1073011|lt=Sagano Line|1=Sagano Line}} to '''Sonobe Station''', then transfer to the '''Keihan Kyoto Kōtsū Bus (Hatta Line)'''. Get off at '''Hatta''' and switch to the '''Gururin Bus'''. Get off at '''Rurikei Bridge''' and walk '''10 minutes'''. == References == === Note === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === References === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=512&r=1505280318.1402 Ōyamazumi Shrine – Kyoto Tourism Federation] * [https://www.city.nantan.kyoto.jp/www/resource/k-map/sonobe/bunka.html Cultural Properties of Sonobe, Nantan City] {{Shinto-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. {{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|version=99835022|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大山祇神社 (南丹市)]] [[ja:別宮大山祇神社]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西の谷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (西海市)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Nantan)}} {{wikidata link|Q43594821}} {{wikidata link|Q43594821}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
